message.content:
Find and read the alternatives.md document in the docs folder:

1. Search for `alternatives.md` in the docs directory
2. Read the entire file, focusing on note-taking app competitors
3. Identify the key features, UX patterns, and design decisions from each competitor
4. Look for any other documentation about Notes features or roadmap

Extract:
- List of note-taking competitors mentioned (Notion, Obsidian, Apple Notes, etc.)
- Key features each competitor has
- Design patterns worth adopting
- Any planned features for Notes in the roadmap

Provide a comprehensive competitive analysis summary.

content.text: Great! I found the alternatives.md file in the tasks folder. Let me read it and also check the related documentation about competitors and Notes features.

content.content:
1→# Competitive Landscape Analysis
     2→
     3→**Last Updated:** December 7, 2025
     4→**Purpose:** Comprehensive competitive analysis across 7+ product categories
     5→**Audience:** Product, marketing, business strategy
     6→**Scope:** 50+ competitors analyzed
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Executive Summary
    11→
    12→Neuman Brain operates in a highly competitive $96.36B productivity software market, competing across **6 overlapping categories** with 50+ established players. Our unique positioning combines features from multiple categories into a single free, privacy-first, local-first platform.
    13→
    14→**Key Finding:** No competitor combines PM tools + habit tracking + gamification while remaining 100% free and privacy-first. This is our blue ocean opportunity.
    15→
    16→### Market Gap Identified
    17→- **Cost Advantage:** Average competitor costs $500-2,000/year; we're $0
    18→- **Speed Advantage:** 60KB bundle vs 2-4MB (10x-40x faster)
    19→- **Privacy Advantage:** Local-first by default vs cloud-only
    20→- **Feature Combination:** Only platform combining professional PM + habit tracking + gamification
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## Product Categories
    25→
    26→Neuman Brain competes in 7 overlapping categories:
    27→
    28→1. **Project/Task Management** (Monday.com, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Jira)
    29→2. **PKM/Notes** (Notion, Obsidian, Logseq, Anytype)
    30→3. **Daily Planning** (Sunsama, Akiflow, Motion)
    31→4. **Time Tracking** (Toggl, Clockify, Harvest)
    32→5. **IT Helpdesk/Ticketing** (Jira Service Desk, Zendesk, Freshdesk)
    33→6. **Habit Tracking** (Habitica, Streaks, Todoist, TickTick)
    34→7. **AI-Native Workspaces** (Refly AI, AFFiNE, Heptabase) - NEW Dec 2025
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Category 1: Project/Task Management
    39→
    40→### Monday.com
    41→- **Pricing:** $12-20/user/month ($144-240/year for 10 people)
    42→- **Strengths:** Visual workflows, automations, integrations
    43→- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, complex UI, cloud-only
    44→- **Our Advantage:** Free, 10x faster, local-first
    45→
    46→### ClickUp
    47→- **Pricing:** $9-19/user/month
    48→- **Strengths:** Feature-rich, customizable
    49→- **Weaknesses:** Overwhelming UI, slow performance, privacy concerns
    50→- **Our Advantage:** Simpler, faster, privacy-first
    51→
    52→### Asana
    53→- **Pricing:** $13.49-30.49/user/month
    54→- **Strengths:** Clean UI, solid collaboration
    55→- **Weaknesses:** Limited free tier, expensive for teams
    56→- **Our Advantage:** Free, open source, local-first
    57→
    58→### Todoist
    59→- **Pricing:** $5/month ($48/year)
    60→- **Strengths:** Simple, fast, good mobile apps
    61→- **Weaknesses:** No projects/time tracking, limited free tier
    62→- **Our Advantage:** More features (time tracking, projects), free
    63→
    64→### Fizzy (fizzy.do)
    65→- **Pricing:** Free for 1,000 cards (lifetime), $20/month unlimited
    66→- **Strengths:** 37signals pedigree (Basecamp makers), vibrant UI, keyboard-first (Do Bar), open source (O'Saasy License), self-hostable, fast, Rails + Hotwire
    67→- **Weaknesses:** Kanban-only (no notes/time tracking), O'Saasy license prevents SaaS competition, Rails stack (less portable)
    68→- **Our Advantage:** Full productivity suite (notes, time tracking, calendar), MIT license (true open source), JavaScript/TypeScript stack, more comprehensive feature set
    69→- **Notable:** Golden Ticket priority system, command-line style UI, anti-muted design philosophy, 3K+ GitHub stars (Dec 2025)
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Category 2: PKM/Notes
    74→
    75→### Notion
    76→- **Pricing:** $10-18/user/month
    77→- **Strengths:** Flexible databases, beautiful UI, collaboration
    78→- **Weaknesses:** Slow (2MB bundle), cloud-only, privacy concerns, $120-216/year
    79→- **Our Advantage:** 10x faster (60KB), local-first, free, privacy-first
    80→
    81→### Obsidian
    82→- **Pricing:** Free (sync $10/month, publish $20/month)
    83→- **Strengths:** Local-first, Markdown, plugins, privacy
    84→- **Weaknesses:** Desktop-first (mobile sync $$$), no integrated tasks/time tracking
    85→- **Our Advantage:** Web-based, integrated productivity suite, free sync options
    86→
    87→### Logseq
    88→- **Pricing:** Free, open source
    89→- **Strengths:** Outliner, local-first, open source
    90→- **Weaknesses:** Desktop-first, complex UX, no time tracking
    91→- **Our Advantage:** Simpler, web-based, integrated tasks + time tracking
    92→
    93→### Anytype
    94→- **Pricing:** Free (decentralized)
    95→- **Strengths:** P2P sync, E2EE, open source
    96→- **Weaknesses:** Alpha stage, complex UX, no time tracking
    97→- **Our Advantage:** Stable, simpler, integrated productivity features
    98→
    99→### Standard Notes
   100→- **Pricing:** $9.99/month ($120/year)
   101→- **Strengths:** E2EE, privacy-first, simple
   102→- **Weaknesses:** Basic features, no tasks/projects, expensive
   103→- **Our Advantage:** Free, more features, planned E2EE
   104→
   105→### Roam Research
   106→- **Pricing:** $15/month ($180/year) 🔥
   107→- **Strengths:** Bidirectional linking, powerful for research
   108→- **Weaknesses:** Very expensive, cloud-only, slow
   109→- **Our Advantage:** Free, faster, local-first
   110→
   111→---
   112→
   113→## Category 3: Daily Planning
   114→
   115→### Sunsama
   116→- **Pricing:** $20/month ($240/year) 🔥
   117→- **Strengths:** Beautiful daily planning UI, integrations
   118→- **Weaknesses:** Extremely expensive, cloud-only
   119→- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy-first, local storage
   120→
   121→### Akiflow
   122→- **Pricing:** $19/month ($228/year)
   123→- **Strengths:** Calendar + tasks integration
   124→- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only
   125→- **Our Advantage:** Free, similar features, local-first
   126→
   127→### Motion
   128→- **Pricing:** $34/month ($408/year) 🔥🔥
   129→- **Strengths:** AI scheduling
   130→- **Weaknesses:** Prohibitively expensive, cloud-only, AI requires server
   131→- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy (no AI tracking), local-first
   132→
   133→---
   134→
   135→## Category 4: Time Tracking
   136→
   137→### Toggl Track
   138→- **Pricing:** $10-20/user/month
   139→- **Strengths:** Simple, good reports
   140→- **Weaknesses:** Expensive for teams, cloud-only
   141→- **Our Advantage:** Free, integrated with tasks, local-first
   142→
   143→### Clockify
   144→- **Pricing:** Free (paid $5-12/user/month for features)
   145→- **Strengths:** Free tier, team features
   146→- **Weaknesses:** Cloud-only, ads in free tier
   147→- **Our Advantage:** No ads, local-first, integrated with notes/tasks
   148→
   149→### Harvest
   150→- **Pricing:** $12/user/month
   151→- **Strengths:** Invoicing integration
   152→- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only, overkill for individuals
   153→- **Our Advantage:** Free, simpler, local-first
   154→
   155→---
   156→
   157→## Category 5: IT Helpdesk/Ticketing
   158→
   159→### Jira Service Desk
   160→- **Pricing:** $22-57/agent/month
   161→- **Strengths:** Enterprise features, Atlassian ecosystem
   162→- **Weaknesses:** Extremely expensive, complex, slow
   163→- **Our Advantage:** Free, simpler, faster
   164→
   165→### Zendesk
   166→- **Pricing:** $19-99/agent/month
   167→- **Strengths:** Mature platform, integrations
   168→- **Weaknesses:** Very expensive, cloud-only
   169→- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy-first, local-first
   170→
   171→### Freshdesk
   172→- **Pricing:** $15-79/agent/month
   173→- **Strengths:** Modern UI, good free tier
   174→- **Weaknesses:** Limited free tier, cloud-only
   175→- **Our Advantage:** Unlimited free, local-first
   176→
   177→---
   178→
   179→## Category 6: Habit Tracking
   180→
   181→### Habitica
   182→- **Pricing:** Free (optional $5/month)
   183→- **Strengths:** Gamification, fun
   184→- **Weaknesses:** Can be gimmicky, cloud-only
   185→- **Our Advantage:** Optional gamification, privacy-first, integrated with tasks
   186→
   187→### Streaks (iOS)
   188→- **Pricing:** $6 one-time
   189→- **Strengths:** Simple, Apple Design Award
   190→- **Weaknesses:** iOS-only, no notes/tasks
   191→- **Our Advantage:** Cross-platform, integrated productivity
   192→
   193→### Loop Habit Tracker (Android)
   194→- **Pricing:** Free, open source
   195→- **Strengths:** Privacy, open source
   196→- **Weaknesses:** Android-only, basic features
   197→- **Our Advantage:** Cross-platform (web), more features
   198→
   199→---
   200→
   201→## Category 7: AI-Native Workspaces (NEW Dec 2025)
   202→
   203→### Refly AI (refly.ai)
   204→- **Pricing:** Freemium (Free + Pro + Team), credit-based, BYOK option
   205→- **Positioning:** "Combines Notion + NotebookLM + Perplexity in one workspace"
   206→- **Launch:** January 2025 | **Current:** v0.10.0 (Dec 2025)
   207→- **GitHub:** 4,900+ stars, 438 forks, 33+ contributors (very active)
   208→- **License:** ReflyAI Open Source License (Apache 2.0 with restrictions)
   209→- **Tech Stack:** TypeScript 97%, React, NestJS, Zustand, Tiptap, ReactFlow, LangChain
   210→- **Strengths:**
   211→  - Free canvas with multi-threaded dialogues (unique feature)
   212→  - 13+ LLM integrations (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini 2.0, DeepSeek R1)
   213→  - Visual "Vibe Workflow" builder (natural language automation)
   214→  - AI knowledge base with RAG semantic search
   215→  - Chrome extension for web clipping
   216→  - Workflow marketplace with creator monetization (20% revenue share)
   217→  - Self-hostable via Docker/Kubernetes
   218→  - Active open-source community
   219→- **Weaknesses:**
   220→  - Cloud-dependent (even self-hosted needs API keys)
   221→  - Credit costs for heavy AI usage
   222→  - "Good enough everywhere, master of none" (per XDA review)
   223→  - Learning curve for non-technical users
   224→  - No task/project management features
   225→  - No time tracking
   226→  - No calendar integration
   227→  - No habit tracking
   228→- **Our Advantage:**
   229→  - True local-first (no server/API dependency)
   230→  - Full productivity suite (tasks, calendar, time tracking)
   231→  - Zero cost (no credits, no subscriptions)
   232→  - Privacy by default (no cloud required)
   233→  - Simpler setup (no API keys needed)
   234→  - Task/project management (they don't have)
   235→  - Habit tracking + gamification (planned, they won't build)
   236→- **Target:** Content creators, researchers, writers
   237→- **Sources:** [XDA](https://www.xda-developers.com/refly-ai-combines-notion-notebooklm-perplexity-in-one-super-productivity-app/), [GitHub](https://github.com/refly-ai/refly)
   238→
   239→### AFFiNE
   240→- **Pricing:** Free, open source
   241→- **Strengths:** Notion + Miro alternative, local-first canvas, block editor
   242→- **Weaknesses:** Still in beta, fewer AI features than Refly
   243→- **Our Advantage:** More stable, integrated productivity features
   244→
   245→### Heptabase
   246→- **Pricing:** $12/month ($144/year)
   247→- **Strengths:** Visual canvas PKM, thinking/learning focused
   248→- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only, niche focus
   249→- **Our Advantage:** Free, integrated task management, local-first
   250→
   251→---
   252→
   253→## Competitive Matrix
   254→
   255→| Competitor | Category | Pricing/Year | Privacy | Speed | Open Source | Our Advantage |
   256→|------------|----------|--------------|---------|-------|-------------|---------------|
   257→| **Notion** | Notes | $120-216 | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | ❌ | 10x faster, free, local-first |
   258→| **Monday.com** | PM | $144-240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, privacy, 10x faster |
   259→| **Obsidian** | Notes | $0-360 | ✅ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Web-based, integrated tasks |
   260→| **Sunsama** | Planning | $240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, privacy-first |
   261→| **Toggl** | Time | $120-240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, integrated tasks |
   262→| **Roam** | Notes | $180 | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, faster, local-first |
   263→| **Fizzy** | Kanban | $0-240 | ✅ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⚠️ O'Saasy | Full suite, MIT license, notes/time |
   264→| **Refly AI** | AI Workspace | $0-? (credits) | ⚠️ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⚠️ Apache+restrictions | No tasks/time, local-first, zero cost |
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## Strategic Positioning
   269→
   270→### Blue Ocean Strategy:
   271→**"Privacy-first, all-in-one productivity for individuals and small teams, zero monthly fees"**
   272→
   273→### Unfair Advantages:
   274→1. **Cost:** $0 vs $500-2,000/year for comparable stack
   275→2. **Speed:** 60KB vs 2MB+ (10x faster)
   276→3. **Privacy:** Local-first by default vs cloud-only
   277→4. **Open Source:** MIT license vs proprietary
   278→5. **No Vendor Lock-In:** Your data, your device
   279→
   280→### Weaknesses to Address:
   281→1. **No mobile apps** (web PWA only)
   282→2. **No real-time collaboration** (coming Q2 2026)
   283→3. **No E2EE yet** (planned Q2 2026)
   284→4. **Notes features missing** (critical gap, Q1 2026)
   285→
   286→---
   287→
   288→## Complete Feature Comparison Matrix
   289→
   290→### All-in-One Platforms
   291→
   292→| Feature | Neuman Brain | Monday.com | ClickUp | Notion | Trello |
   293→|---------|--------------|------------|---------|--------|--------|

   371→8. **Calendar Sync** - Integrate with existing workflows (Google Calendar, Outlook)

   376→## Positioning Statements

   386→- "All your productivity tools in one place: tasks, calendar, habits, time tracking. Forever free."

   394→| **Habit Builder** | "Habitica with real project management features" | Habitica |
   395→| **Freelancer** | "Toggl + Asana combined, $0 instead of $360/year" | Toggl + Asana |
   396→| **Privacy-Conscious** | "Local-first Notion alternative, your data stays yours" | Notion |
   397→| **Knowledge Worker** | "Refly's AI research + task/time tracking, zero credits" | Refly AI (credits) |
   398→
   399→---
   400→
   401→## Threat Analysis
   402→
   403→### High Threats
   404→1. **ClickUp adds gamification** - They move fast, well-funded
   405→   - **Mitigation:** Ship habits + gamification in Sprint 2 (before they notice)
   406→
   407→2. **Monday.com launches free tier** - They have resources
   408→   - **Mitigation:** Emphasize privacy, speed, open source (they can't match)
   409→
   410→3. **Habitica improves PM features** - Open source, could copy our roadmap
   411→   - **Mitigation:** Better UI, faster execution, professional focus
   412→

   414→4. **Notion adds Gantt charts** - Completing PM feature set

   422→

# Competitive Landscape Analysis

**Last Updated:** December 7, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive competitive analysis across 7+ product categories
**Audience:** Product, marketing, business strategy
**Scope:** 50+ competitors analyzed

---

## Executive Summary

Neuman Brain operates in a highly competitive $96.36B productivity software market, competing across **6 overlapping categories** with 50+ established players. Our unique positioning combines features from multiple categories into a single free, privacy-first, local-first platform.

**Key Finding:** No competitor combines PM tools + habit tracking + gamification while remaining 100% free and privacy-first. This is our blue ocean opportunity.

### Market Gap Identified
- **Cost Advantage:** Average competitor costs $500-2,000/year; we're $0
- **Speed Advantage:** 60KB bundle vs 2-4MB (10x-40x faster)
- **Privacy Advantage:** Local-first by default vs cloud-only
- **Feature Combination:** Only platform combining professional PM + habit tracking + gamification

---

## Product Categories

Neuman Brain competes in 7 overlapping categories:

1. **Project/Task Management** (Monday.com, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Jira)
2. **PKM/Notes** (Notion, Obsidian, Logseq, Anytype)
3. **Daily Planning** (Sunsama, Akiflow, Motion)
4. **Time Tracking** (Toggl, Clockify, Harvest)
5. **IT Helpdesk/Ticketing** (Jira Service Desk, Zendesk, Freshdesk)
6. **Habit Tracking** (Habitica, Streaks, Todoist, TickTick)
7. **AI-Native Workspaces** (Refly AI, AFFiNE, Heptabase) - NEW Dec 2025

---

## Category 1: Project/Task Management

### Monday.com
- **Pricing:** $12-20/user/month ($144-240/year for 10 people)
- **Strengths:** Visual workflows, automations, integrations
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, complex UI, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, 10x faster, local-first

### ClickUp
- **Pricing:** $9-19/user/month
- **Strengths:** Feature-rich, customizable
- **Weaknesses:** Overwhelming UI, slow performance, privacy concerns
- **Our Advantage:** Simpler, faster, privacy-first

### Asana
- **Pricing:** $13.49-30.49/user/month
- **Strengths:** Clean UI, solid collaboration
- **Weaknesses:** Limited free tier, expensive for teams
- **Our Advantage:** Free, open source, local-first

### Todoist
- **Pricing:** $5/month ($48/year)
- **Strengths:** Simple, fast, good mobile apps
- **Weaknesses:** No projects/time tracking, limited free tier
- **Our Advantage:** More features (time tracking, projects), free

### Fizzy (fizzy.do)
- **Pricing:** Free for 1,000 cards (lifetime), $20/month unlimited
- **Strengths:** 37signals pedigree (Basecamp makers), vibrant UI, keyboard-first (Do Bar), open source (O'Saasy License), self-hostable, fast, Rails + Hotwire
- **Weaknesses:** Kanban-only (no notes/time tracking), O'Saasy license prevents SaaS competition, Rails stack (less portable)
- **Our Advantage:** Full productivity suite (notes, time tracking, calendar), MIT license (true open source), JavaScript/TypeScript stack, more comprehensive feature set
- **Notable:** Golden Ticket priority system, command-line style UI, anti-muted design philosophy, 3K+ GitHub stars (Dec 2025)

---

## Category 2: PKM/Notes

### Notion
- **Pricing:** $10-18/user/month
- **Strengths:** Flexible databases, beautiful UI, collaboration
- **Weaknesses:** Slow (2MB bundle), cloud-only, privacy concerns, $120-216/year
- **Our Advantage:** 10x faster (60KB), local-first, free, privacy-first

### Obsidian
- **Pricing:** Free (sync $10/month, publish $20/month)
- **Strengths:** Local-first, Markdown, plugins, privacy
- **Weaknesses:** Desktop-first (mobile sync $$$), no integrated tasks/time tracking
- **Our Advantage:** Web-based, integrated productivity suite, free sync options

### Logseq
- **Pricing:** Free, open source
- **Strengths:** Outliner, local-first, open source
- **Weaknesses:** Desktop-first, complex UX, no time tracking
- **Our Advantage:** Simpler, web-based, integrated tasks + time tracking

### Anytype
- **Pricing:** Free (decentralized)
- **Strengths:** P2P sync, E2EE, open source
- **Weaknesses:** Alpha stage, complex UX, no time tracking
- **Our Advantage:** Stable, simpler, integrated productivity features

### Standard Notes
- **Pricing:** $9.99/month ($120/year)
- **Strengths:** E2EE, privacy-first, simple
- **Weaknesses:** Basic features, no tasks/projects, expensive
- **Our Advantage:** Free, more features, planned E2EE

### Roam Research
- **Pricing:** $15/month ($180/year) 🔥
- **Strengths:** Bidirectional linking, powerful for research
- **Weaknesses:** Very expensive, cloud-only, slow
- **Our Advantage:** Free, faster, local-first

---

## Category 3: Daily Planning

### Sunsama
- **Pricing:** $20/month ($240/year) 🔥
- **Strengths:** Beautiful daily planning UI, integrations
- **Weaknesses:** Extremely expensive, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy-first, local storage

### Akiflow
- **Pricing:** $19/month ($228/year)
- **Strengths:** Calendar + tasks integration
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, similar features, local-first

### Motion
- **Pricing:** $34/month ($408/year) 🔥🔥
- **Strengths:** AI scheduling
- **Weaknesses:** Prohibitively expensive, cloud-only, AI requires server
- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy (no AI tracking), local-first

---

## Category 4: Time Tracking

### Toggl Track
- **Pricing:** $10-20/user/month
- **Strengths:** Simple, good reports
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive for teams, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, integrated with tasks, local-first

### Clockify
- **Pricing:** Free (paid $5-12/user/month for features)
- **Strengths:** Free tier, team features
- **Weaknesses:** Cloud-only, ads in free tier
- **Our Advantage:** No ads, local-first, integrated with notes/tasks

### Harvest
- **Pricing:** $12/user/month
- **Strengths:** Invoicing integration
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only, overkill for individuals
- **Our Advantage:** Free, simpler, local-first

---

## Category 5: IT Helpdesk/Ticketing

### Jira Service Desk
- **Pricing:** $22-57/agent/month
- **Strengths:** Enterprise features, Atlassian ecosystem
- **Weaknesses:** Extremely expensive, complex, slow
- **Our Advantage:** Free, simpler, faster

### Zendesk
- **Pricing:** $19-99/agent/month
- **Strengths:** Mature platform, integrations
- **Weaknesses:** Very expensive, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Free, privacy-first, local-first

### Freshdesk
- **Pricing:** $15-79/agent/month
- **Strengths:** Modern UI, good free tier
- **Weaknesses:** Limited free tier, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Unlimited free, local-first

---

## Category 6: Habit Tracking

### Habitica
- **Pricing:** Free (optional $5/month)
- **Strengths:** Gamification, fun
- **Weaknesses:** Can be gimmicky, cloud-only
- **Our Advantage:** Optional gamification, privacy-first, integrated with tasks

### Streaks (iOS)
- **Pricing:** $6 one-time
- **Strengths:** Simple, Apple Design Award
- **Weaknesses:** iOS-only, no notes/tasks
- **Our Advantage:** Cross-platform, integrated productivity

### Loop Habit Tracker (Android)
- **Pricing:** Free, open source
- **Strengths:** Privacy, open source
- **Weaknesses:** Android-only, basic features
- **Our Advantage:** Cross-platform (web), more features

---

## Category 7: AI-Native Workspaces (NEW Dec 2025)

### Refly AI (refly.ai)
- **Pricing:** Freemium (Free + Pro + Team), credit-based, BYOK option
- **Positioning:** "Combines Notion + NotebookLM + Perplexity in one workspace"
- **Launch:** January 2025 | **Current:** v0.10.0 (Dec 2025)
- **GitHub:** 4,900+ stars, 438 forks, 33+ contributors (very active)
- **License:** ReflyAI Open Source License (Apache 2.0 with restrictions)
- **Tech Stack:** TypeScript 97%, React, NestJS, Zustand, Tiptap, ReactFlow, LangChain
- **Strengths:**
  - Free canvas with multi-threaded dialogues (unique feature)
  - 13+ LLM integrations (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini 2.0, DeepSeek R1)
  - Visual "Vibe Workflow" builder (natural language automation)
  - AI knowledge base with RAG semantic search
  - Chrome extension for web clipping
  - Workflow marketplace with creator monetization (20% revenue share)
  - Self-hostable via Docker/Kubernetes
  - Active open-source community
- **Weaknesses:**
  - Cloud-dependent (even self-hosted needs API keys)
  - Credit costs for heavy AI usage
  - "Good enough everywhere, master of none" (per XDA review)
  - Learning curve for non-technical users
  - No task/project management features
  - No time tracking
  - No calendar integration
  - No habit tracking
- **Our Advantage:**
  - True local-first (no server/API dependency)
  - Full productivity suite (tasks, calendar, time tracking)
  - Zero cost (no credits, no subscriptions)
  - Privacy by default (no cloud required)
  - Simpler setup (no API keys needed)
  - Task/project management (they don't have)
  - Habit tracking + gamification (planned, they won't build)
- **Target:** Content creators, researchers, writers
- **Sources:** [XDA](https://www.xda-developers.com/refly-ai-combines-notion-notebooklm-perplexity-in-one-super-productivity-app/), [GitHub](https://github.com/refly-ai/refly)

### AFFiNE
- **Pricing:** Free, open source
- **Strengths:** Notion + Miro alternative, local-first canvas, block editor
- **Weaknesses:** Still in beta, fewer AI features than Refly
- **Our Advantage:** More stable, integrated productivity features

### Heptabase
- **Pricing:** $12/month ($144/year)
- **Strengths:** Visual canvas PKM, thinking/learning focused
- **Weaknesses:** Expensive, cloud-only, niche focus
- **Our Advantage:** Free, integrated task management, local-first

---

## Competitive Matrix

| Competitor | Category | Pricing/Year | Privacy | Speed | Open Source | Our Advantage |
|------------|----------|--------------|---------|-------|-------------|---------------|
| **Notion** | Notes | $120-216 | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | ❌ | 10x faster, free, local-first |
| **Monday.com** | PM | $144-240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, privacy, 10x faster |
| **Obsidian** | Notes | $0-360 | ✅ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Web-based, integrated tasks |
| **Sunsama** | Planning | $240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, privacy-first |
| **Toggl** | Time | $120-240 | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, integrated tasks |
| **Roam** | Notes | $180 | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | ❌ | Free, faster, local-first |
| **Fizzy** | Kanban | $0-240 | ✅ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⚠️ O'Saasy | Full suite, MIT license, notes/time |
| **Refly AI** | AI Workspace | $0-? (credits) | ⚠️ | ⭐⭐⭐ | ⚠️ Apache+restrictions | No tasks/time, local-first, zero cost |

---

## Strategic Positioning

### Blue Ocean Strategy:
**"Privacy-first, all-in-one productivity for individuals and small teams, zero monthly fees"**

### Unfair Advantages:
1. **Cost:** $0 vs $500-2,000/year for comparable stack
2. **Speed:** 60KB vs 2MB+ (10x faster)
3. **Privacy:** Local-first by default vs cloud-only
4. **Open Source:** MIT license vs proprietary
5. **No Vendor Lock-In:** Your data, your device

### Weaknesses to Address:
1. **No mobile apps** (web PWA only)
2. **No real-time collaboration** (coming Q2 2026)
3. **No E2EE yet** (planned Q2 2026)
4. **Notes features missing** (critical gap, Q1 2026)

---

## Complete Feature Comparison Matrix

### All-in-One Platforms

| Feature | Neuman Brain | Monday.com | ClickUp | Notion | Trello |
|---------|--------------|------------|---------|--------|--------|
| **Price (10 users/year)** | **$0** | $1,440+ | $840+ | $1,200+ | $600+ |
| **Kanban Board** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Calendar View** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ (paid) |
| **Gantt Charts** | ⏳ Sprint 5 | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ⚠️ Paid |
| **Time Tracking** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ⚠️ Via apps | ✅ | ❌ | ⚠️ Via Power-Ups |
| **Recurring Tasks** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Habit Tracking** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ❌ | ⚠️ Basic | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Streaks** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Gamification (XP/Levels)** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Collaboration** | ⏳ Sprint 4 | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅ | ✅✅ | ✅ |
| **Mobile App** | ⏳ PWA Sprint 1 | ✅ Native | ✅ Native | ✅ Native | ✅ Native |
| **Notifications** | ⏳ Sprint 1 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Templates** | ⏳ Sprint 3 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅✅✅ | ⚠️ Limited |
| **Search** | ⏳ Sprint 1 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅✅ | ✅ |
| **Automations** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ Basic | ✅ Butler |
| **Analytics** | ⏳ Sprint 3 | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅ | ⚠️ Basic | ⚠️ Paid |
| **API** | ⏳ Sprint 6 | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Offline Mode** | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ⚠️ Limited | ❌ |
| **Open Source** | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Local-First** | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Bundle Size** | ✅ 60KB | ❌ 3MB+ | ❌ 4MB+ | ❌ 2MB+ | ⚠️ 1MB |

**Legend:**
- ✅ = Has feature
- ✅✅ = Good implementation
- ✅✅✅ = Excellent/best-in-class
- ⚠️ = Limited/basic/paid
- ❌ = Doesn't have
- ⏳ = Planned (Sprint X)

### Habit & Productivity Apps

| Feature | Neuman Brain | Habitica | Todoist | TickTick |
|---------|--------------|----------|---------|----------|
| **Price/Year** | **$0** | $0-60 | $48 | $28 |
| **Task Management** | ✅ | ⚠️ Basic | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Kanban Board** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **Calendar View** | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **Habit Tracking** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ✅✅✅ | ⚠️ Basic streaks | ✅ |
| **Gamification** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ✅✅✅ XP/Levels/Gear | ⚠️ Karma points | ❌ |
| **Streaks** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ✅ | ⚠️ Paid only | ❌ |
| **Time Tracking** | ⏳ Sprint 2 | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ Pomodoro |
| **Collaboration** | ⏳ Sprint 4 | ⚠️ Parties | ⚠️ Limited | ❌ |
| **Gantt Charts** | ⏳ Sprint 5 | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| **Offline Mode** | ✅ | ⚠️ Limited | ⚠️ Limited | ⚠️ Limited |
| **Privacy-First** | ✅ Local storage | ❌ Cloud | ❌ Cloud | ❌ Cloud |

### Competitive Scoring Summary

| Platform | Total Features | Price | Privacy | Speed | **Overall Score** |
|----------|---------------|-------|---------|-------|-------------------|
| **Neuman Brain (Current)** | 10/20 (50%) | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | **7/10** |
| **Neuman Brain (Q4 2026)** | 18/20 (90%) | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | **9.5/10** |
| Monday.com | 18/20 (90%) | ❌ | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | 7/10 |
| ClickUp | 19/20 (95%) | ❌ | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | 6.5/10 |
| Notion | 14/20 (70%) | ❌ | ❌ | ⭐⭐ | 5/10 |
| Habitica | 8/20 (40%) | ✅ | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐ | 5.5/10 |
| Todoist | 12/20 (60%) | ⚠️ | ❌ | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 6/10 |

**Target Position (Q4 2026):** Beat all competitors on cost, privacy, and speed while matching 90% of features.

---

## Strategic Recommendations

### Immediate Priorities (Sprint 1-2)
1. **Close Mobile Gap** - PWA + Notifications (100% of competitors have mobile apps)
2. **Launch Habits + Gamification** - Unique differentiator vs PM tools, competitive with Habitica
3. **Add Time Tracking** - Essential for knowledge workers, free alternative to Toggl ($120-240/year)

### Mid-Term (Sprint 3-4)
4. **Templates Library** - 95% of competitors have this, critical for activation
5. **Team Collaboration** - Required for team adoption and viral growth
6. **Analytics Dashboard** - Data lock-in mechanism (85% of competitors have this)

### Long-Term (Sprint 5-6)
7. **Gantt Charts** - Compete with MS Project and Monday.com for professional users
8. **Calendar Sync** - Integrate with existing workflows (Google Calendar, Outlook)
9. **API & Integrations** - Ecosystem play, developer adoption

---

## Positioning Statements

### For Marketing

**Primary:**
> "The only productivity platform combining professional project management, habit tracking, and gamification — completely free, open source, and privacy-first."

**Alternatives:**
- "Monday.com's power + Habitica's gamification, zero monthly fees"
- "The free, privacy-first alternative to Monday.com, Notion, and Habitica combined"
- "All your productivity tools in one place: tasks, calendar, habits, time tracking. Forever free."

### Competitive Positioning by Use Case

| User Type | Positioning | Primary Competitor |
|-----------|-------------|-------------------|
| **Solo Developer** | "Todoist with time tracking and habits, completely free" | Todoist ($48/yr) |
| **Small Team (5-10)** | "Monday.com without the $1,440/year price tag" | Monday.com |
| **Habit Builder** | "Habitica with real project management features" | Habitica |
| **Freelancer** | "Toggl + Asana combined, $0 instead of $360/year" | Toggl + Asana |
| **Privacy-Conscious** | "Local-first Notion alternative, your data stays yours" | Notion |
| **Knowledge Worker** | "Refly's AI research + task/time tracking, zero credits" | Refly AI (credits) |

---

## Threat Analysis

### High Threats
1. **ClickUp adds gamification** - They move fast, well-funded
   - **Mitigation:** Ship habits + gamification in Sprint 2 (before they notice)

2. **Monday.com launches free tier** - They have resources
   - **Mitigation:** Emphasize privacy, speed, open source (they can't match)

3. **Habitica improves PM features** - Open source, could copy our roadmap
   - **Mitigation:** Better UI, faster execution, professional focus

### Medium Threats
4. **Notion adds Gantt charts** - Completing PM feature set
   - **Mitigation:** We'll have Gantt in Sprint 5, plus habits (they won't add)

5. **New well-funded competitor** - Hot space, VCs funding
   - **Mitigation:** Community moat (open source), speed advantage

6. **Fizzy (37signals) expands beyond Kanban** - Strong brand, Rails expertise
   - **Mitigation:** We offer full productivity suite they won't build; their O'Saasy license limits ecosystem; we're web-native (React) vs Rails

7. **Refly AI gains traction** - 4.9K GitHub stars, rapid iteration (v0.10 in <1 year), AI-first positioning
   - **Assessment:** Different target audience (content creators vs. knowledge workers)
   - **Mitigation:** Emphasize productivity features they lack (tasks, calendar, time tracking, habits); highlight zero-cost vs credit system; maintain local-first advantage as differentiator
   - **Opportunity:** Consider adding canvas/visual brainstorming features; learn from their multi-model AI integration approach

### Low Threats
8. **MS Project lowers price** - Unlikely, enterprise-focused
9. **Trello major redesign** - Atlassian moves slowly
10. **Todoist adds Gantt** - Not their focus

---

**Related Documentation:**
- [Marketing Claims vs Reality](marketing_claims_vs_reality.md) - What we can/can't claim
- [Product Vision](../product/product_vision.md) - Strategic direction
- [Feature Roadmap](../product/feature_roadmap.md) - Implementation timeline
- [Business Model](../business/business_model.md) - Monetization strategy

---

**Document Status:** ✅ Active & Comprehensive
**Last Updated:** December 7, 2025
**Next Review:** March 2026 (Quarterly)
**Owner:** Product Management

   988→### 15.1 Rob Dyrdek's "Machine Mindset" & Existence Platform 💡 P2 - 2027
   989→**Philosophy:** Design time, don't just manage it. Life is a system to optimize.
   990→
   991→**Platform Reference:** Rob Dyrdek's **Existence** (launched November 2025)
   992→- "Time intelligence platform" that implements the Machine Mindset philosophy
   993→- Tags activities as Life/Work/Health/Sleep with time block tracking
   994→- Uncovers "hidden patterns shaping your life" through qualitative tracking
   995→- **Market Validation:** Major celebrity entrepreneur just launched exactly what we're building!

  1001→- **Life Rhythms:** Weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence
  1002→- **"Rhythm of Existence" Document:** Guide covering time, health, money, family/work integration

  1233→## Appendix: Feature Parity Methodology
  1234→
  1235→### How Parity Percentages Are Calculated
  1236→
  1237→**Objective:** Provide transparent, verifiable parity calculations to set accurate user expectations.
  1238→
  1239→**Formula:**
  1240→```
  1241→Parity % = (Shipped Features / Competitor Baseline Features) × 100
  1242→```
  1243→
  1244→**Competitor Baseline:** Count of essential features in the competitor's core product tier (not enterprise-only features).
  1245→

  1534→**Why 71% is accurate:** Phase 3 added all core professional features: manual entry, projects, monthly reports, CSV export, calendar view, and bulk operations. We now have feature parity with Toggl Free tier. Missing only advanced billing (hourly rates, invoicing) and team features.
  1535→
  1536→---
  1537→
  1538→#### AI Terminal: 60% ChatGPT Parity
  1539→
  1540→**Calculation:**
  1541→- **ChatGPT Baseline:** 25 core features
  1542→- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 15 features
  1543→- **Parity:** 15/25 = 60%
  1544→
  1545→**Missing features (10 total):**
  1546→- Voice input
  1547→- Voice output
  1548→- Image generation (DALL-E)
  1549→- Web browsing mode
  1550→- Code interpreter
  1551→- File uploads
  1552→- Custom instructions
  1553→- Conversation folders
  1554→- Shared conversations
  1555→- ChatGPT plugins
  1556→
  1557→**Why 60% is accurate:** We have multi-provider support (8 providers vs ChatGPT's 1), encryption, usage tracking, and auto-fallback. Missing advanced features like voice, images, and plugins.
  1558→
  1559→---
  1560→
  1561→### Verification Process
  1562→
  1563→**How to verify these calculations:**
  1564→
  1565→1. **Open competitor product** (Obsidian, Notion, Jira, etc.)
  1566→2. **List all core features** in basic tier (exclude enterprise/plugins)
  1567→3. **Count features** in each category
  1568→4. **Check Neuman Brain codebase:**
  1569→   - Search for implementations
  1570→   - Verify in UI (npm run dev)
  1571→   - Check task files for "Shipped" status
  1572→5. **Calculate:** (Shipped / Total) × 100
  1573→
  1574→**Example for Kanban:**
  1575→```bash
  1576→# Search for subtask implementation
  1577→grep -r "subtask" src/widgets/Kanban/
  1578→# Found: SubtaskList.tsx, subtask creation logic, progress tracking
  1579→
  1580→# Search for dependencies
  1581→grep -r "dependencies" src/widgets/Kanban/
  1582→# Found: dependencies array, blocker logic, visualization
  1583→
  1584→# Search for ListView
  1585→ls src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx
  1586→# Found: Complete ListView implementation with sorting, filtering, bulk ops
  1587→```
  1588→
  1589→**Result:** 49/50 features verified in codebase = 98% parity ✅
  1590→
  1591→---
  1592→
  1593→### Transparency Note
  1594→
  1595→**Why we document methodology:**
  1596→
  1597→1. **Set accurate expectations** - Users know exactly what we have vs don't have
  1598→2. **No marketing fluff** - Every percentage is verifiable via codebase
  1599→3. **Track progress** - Easy to update as features ship
  1600→4. **Honest comparisons** - We don't inflate numbers or cherry-pick competitors
  1601→5. **Engineering rigor** - Treat documentation with same precision as code
  1602→
  1603→**Philosophy:** If we claim 98% Kanban parity, you should be able to verify all 49 shipped features by reading the code. If we can't defend a number with evidence, we don't publish it.
  1604→
  1605→---
  1606→
  1607→**Related:**
  1608→[Product Roadmap](feature_roadmap.md) - Phased delivery plan
  1609→[User Gaps](../strategy/user_expectations_gaps.md) - Feature gaps
  1610→[Implementation Plan](../technical/implementation_plan.md) - Sprint breakdown
  1611→[CHANGELOG](../CHANGELOG.md) - Release history
  1612→
  1613→**Document Status:** ✅ Active
  1614→**Last Updated:** December 14, 2025
  1615→

# Feature Map & Taxonomy

**Last Updated:** December 14, 2025
**Purpose:** Complete feature inventory and categorization
**Audience:** Product, engineering, design

---

## Feature Status Legend

✅ **Shipped** - Live in production
🚧 **In Progress** - Currently being developed
📋 **Planned** - Roadmapped with timeline
💡 **Idea** - Backlog, no timeline
❌ **Not Planned** - Out of scope

---

## 1. Notes & Knowledge Management

### 1.1 Core Notes (P0 - Q1 2026) ✅ SHIPPED November 2025
- ✅ Rich text editor (Lexical) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Markdown editing mode - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Folder hierarchy (unlimited depth) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Note creation/deletion with undo - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Full-text search with UI (Cmd+K) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Auto-save (debounced, IndexedDB) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ❌ Version history - Not implemented
- ✅ Note templates - Shipped Nov 2025

**Parity:** 55% Obsidian, 50% Notion (vs original goal 30%/40%)

**Research:** See `docs/product/notes-page-research.md` for comprehensive competitive analysis (15 apps) and prioritized improvement roadmap (12 features across 3 phases).

### 1.2 Formatting (P1 - Q1-Q2 2026) ✅ SHIPPED November 2025
- ✅ Bold, italic, underline - Shipped Nov 2025 (keyboard shortcuts work)
- ✅ Headings (H1-H3 via slash commands) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Lists (bullet, numbered via slash commands) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Code blocks (via slash /code) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Blockquotes (via slash /quote) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Tables (3x3 via TablePlugin) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ❌ Horizontal rules - Not implemented
- ❌ Text colors/highlights - Not implemented
- ❌ Strikethrough - Not implemented

**Additional Features:**
- ✅ Slash commands (/heading, /list, /code, /quote) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Markdown auto-transform (## → heading, * → bullet) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Auto-linking (URLs become clickable) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Export to Markdown (.md download) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Word/character count (live status bar) - Shipped Jan 2026

### 1.3 Media & Embeds (P1-P2 - Q2 2026)
- ❌ Inline images (drag-and-drop) - Phase 5 planned
- ❌ Image resizing
- ❌ File attachments (PDFs, etc.)
- ❌ YouTube embeds
- ❌ Web bookmarks (link previews)
- 💡 Audio recordings
- 💡 Video embeds

### 1.4 Linking & Organization (P1-P2 - Q2 2026)
- ✅ **[[Wiki-style links]] with Autocomplete** - Shipped Nov 29, 2025 (Phase 2.2)
  - Type `[[` to trigger autocomplete dropdown
  - Fuzzy search with relevance scoring (exact match, starts with, contains, fuzzy)
  - Keyboard navigation (↑↓ Enter Esc)
  - Highlight matching characters in results
  - Show folder paths for context
  - Smart boosting (same folder +10, recent notes +0-5)
  - Max 10 results for performance
  - Click or Enter to insert `[[Note Title]]`
  - **Parity with Obsidian/Roam Research baseline linking** ✅
- ✅ **Backlinks Panel** - Shipped Nov 2025 (Phase 2.5)
  - Linked References: Notes that link to current note
  - Unlinked Mentions: Text mentions without wiki-links
  - Broken Links: Links to non-existent notes with one-click creation
  - Real-time sync with note content
  - Collapsible sections with counts
- ✅ **Nested Tags (Hierarchical Organization)** - Shipped Nov 29, 2025 (Phase 2.4)
  - Support `/` separator for hierarchy (e.g., `projects/work/client-a`)
  - Hierarchical tree display in TagPicker with expand/collapse
  - Parent tags show child count
  - Indentation by level (16px per level)
  - Search mode shows flat list, browse mode shows tree
  - Parent tag filtering includes all child notes
  - Backwards compatible - existing flat tags work unchanged
  - Tag normalization (removes invalid slashes)
  - **Parity with Obsidian nested tags** ✅
- ❌ Tag browser - Planned Phase 2.5
- ✅ **Graph View with Focus Mode & Visual Analytics** - Shipped Nov 29-30, 2025 (Phase 2.3)
  - Interactive D3 force-directed graph visualization
  - Search, tag, and folder filters
  - Hide orphan nodes toggle
  - **Focus Mode:** Click node → shows only N-hop neighborhood (1-3 hops)
  - Depth slider for controlling connection distance
  - Focused node highlighted (gold color, larger size, accent border)
  - Single-click to focus, double-click to navigate
  - URL params for shareable focused views (`/graph?focus=note-id`)
  - LocalStorage persistence (focus & visual settings restored on refresh)
  - Smooth D3 transitions and animations
  - **Visual Analytics (P1):** - Shipped Nov 30, 2025
    - **Node sizing by connections:** Nodes scale 10-30px based on link count (hub nodes larger)
    - **Color grouping:** Group by folder or tag with auto-generated color palette
    - Toggle controls for size/color features (default: size ON, color OFF)
    - Color legend shows active groups with node counts
    - Tooltips show connection count, primary tag, and folder
    - **Parity with Obsidian/Logseq graph analytics** ✅
  - **Link Strength Visualization (P2):** - Shipped Nov 30, 2025
    - **Strong links (3px):** Bidirectional wiki links (A→B and B→A)
    - **Medium links (2px):** Single direction wiki links
    - **Weak links (1px dashed):** Tag-based connections
    - Toggle control for link strength visualization
    - Visual legend explaining strength levels
    - Opacity variations based on strength (0.8/0.6/0.4)
  - **Advanced Graph Search (P2):** - Shipped Nov 30, 2025
    - Text search with debouncing (300ms)
    - Multi-select tag filtering
    - Node type filter (Notes/Tags/Both)
    - Advanced filters panel (collapsible)
    - Active filter chips with quick clear
    - Result count indicator
    - Search highlighting (scale 1.2x, dim non-matches to 0.3 opacity)
  - **Orphan Detection & Suggestions (P2):** - Shipped Nov 30, 2025
    - Automatic detection of isolated notes (no links/tags)
    - Visual markers (magenta fill, orange border)
    - Sidebar panel listing all orphans
    - AI-powered connection suggestions with confidence scores:
      - Similar title (Levenshtein distance > 50%)
      - Same folder (70% confidence)
      - Recent edit proximity (60% confidence)
      - Similar content via word overlap (20%+ match)
      - Common tags in folder (50% confidence)
    - Quick actions: one-click link creation or tag addition
    - Orphan counter badge in toolbar
  - **Parity with Obsidian local graph feature** ✅
- 💡 Aliases
- 💡 Note properties/metadata

### 1.5 Advanced PKM (P3 - 2027)
- 💡 Daily notes
- 💡 Spaced repetition (flashcards)
- 💡 Query/dataview (Notion-like databases)
- 💡 Canvas (visual whiteboard)
- 💡 Excalidraw integration

### 1.6 UX & Interactions (P0 - Q1 2026) ✅ SHIPPED November 2025
- ✅ Modal dialog system (ConfirmDialog, PromptDialog) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ Undo system for delete operations - Shipped Nov 2025
  - Delete notes with 10-second undo window
  - Delete folders with cascading note deletion + undo
  - Toast notifications with countdown timer
  - Future: Kanban tasks, calendar events, bulk operations
- ✅ Folder management (create, rename, delete, drag-and-drop) - Shipped Nov 2025
- ✅ In-app confirmations (no browser pop-ups) - Shipped Nov 2025
- 📋 Keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+Z/Ctrl+Z for undo)
- 💡 Redo functionality (Cmd+Shift+Z/Ctrl+Shift+Z)
- 💡 Persistent undo history (cross-session)

### 1.7 Notes Polish - Phase 1 Quick Wins ✅ SHIPPED November 28, 2025
- ✅ **Typography & Spacing** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Increased line-height to 1.625 (leading-relaxed) for readability
  - Max-width 70ch for optimal reading experience
  - Improved heading sizes and spacing (H1: text-4xl, H2: text-3xl, H3: text-2xl)
  - Better padding around editor content (p-6)
- ✅ **Visual Hierarchy** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Enhanced code inline styling with padding (px-1.5 py-0.5)
  - Styled blockquotes with accent-colored left border + subtle background
  - Improved code block styling with shadow and darker background
  - Link styling with accent colors and smooth transitions
  - List spacing improvements (space-y-1.5)
- ✅ **Folder Sidebar Polish** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Smooth transitions on hover (duration-standard)
  - Active folder highlight with accent colors (cyan/magenta)
  - Hover states with shadow effects
  - Improved text color transitions on hover
- ✅ **Loading States & Micro-interactions** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Fade-in animations on note load (animate-fade-in)
  - Subtle scale effect on note list items (hover:scale-[1.01])
  - Smooth transitions across all interactive elements (duration-standard)
- ✅ **Empty States** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Beautiful gradient backgrounds for icons (magenta/cyan gradients)
  - Context-aware empty state messages (folder vs tags)
  - Keyboard shortcut hints (Cmd+N, Cmd+K)
  - Welcoming copy emphasizing privacy and local-first
  - Professional empty state design with better spacing and hierarchy

**Impact:** Addresses CRITICAL user feedback "unpolished and childish feeling"
**Time Invested:** ~3 hours (Quick Wins as planned)
**Next Phases:** Notes Polish Phase 2 (4-6 hours) - Command Palette, Preview Mode, Focus Mode

### 1.8 Bookmark Management - Enhanced Import ✅ SHIPPED November 28, 2025
- ✅ **Drag-Drop HTML Import** - Shipped (already existed)
  - Accepts bookmark files via drag-and-drop
  - Validates Netscape bookmark format
  - Provides import feedback with stats
- ✅ **Smart Merge - Duplicate Detection** - Shipped Nov 28, 2025
  - Detects duplicates by URL before importing
  - Skips existing bookmarks automatically
  - Shows duplicate count in import stats
  - Prevents duplicate entries on re-import
  - Efficient URL indexing with Set for O(1) lookup
- 📋 **Export Guide** - Deferred to Phase 2
  - Browser-specific export instructions
  - Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge guidance
  - Step-by-step UI modal (planned)

**Impact:** Prevents bookmark duplicates, better import UX
**Time Invested:** 1 hour (Smart Merge implementation)
**Next Phases:** Browser Extension (12-16h) - Full sync solution from research plan

---

## 2. Tasks & Projects

### 2.1 Task Management (Shipped ✅)
- ✅ Kanban board (drag-and-drop)
- ✅ Task creation/editing/deletion
- ✅ Due dates
- ✅ Column-based priorities (To Do, In Progress, Done)
- ✅ Task descriptions
- ✅ Calendar integration
- ✅ **Subtasks with rich properties** (Shipped Nov 2025 - Phase 5)
  - Nested tasks with title, description, priority, due date
  - Completion tracking with progress bars
  - Drag-and-drop reordering
  - Full CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)
  - Three-color badge system (green/blue/gray for progress)
- ✅ **Professional Task Dependencies** (Shipped Nov 2025 - Phase 1.1) ✅
  - **4 Dependency Types:** FS (Finish-to-Start), SS (Start-to-Start), FF (Finish-to-Finish), SF (Start-to-Finish)
  - **Lag Time Support:** Positive (delay) and negative (lead/overlap) in days
  - DependencyPicker UI with visual type selection and descriptions
  - Circular dependency prevention with cycle detection
  - Gantt Timeline visualization with different arrow styles per type (solid, dashed, dotted, dash-dot)
  - Hover tooltips showing dependency type and lag time
  - Automatic migration from simple "Blocked By/Blocks" format
  - **Parity with MS Project, ClickUp, Monday.com** ✅
- ✅ **Custom columns** (Shipped Nov 2025 - Phase 4)
  - Fully customizable workflow stages
  - Add/edit/delete/reorder columns
  - Drag tasks between custom stages
  - **Current Parity: 100% Jira/Linear PM features** ✅
- ✅ **Team Members & Assignees** (Shipped Nov 2025 - Phase 3.1)
  - Team member management in Settings (add/edit/delete members)
  - Member profiles with name, email, avatar color, and initials

## 15. Strategic Frameworks & Influences 💡

### 15.1 Rob Dyrdek's "Machine Mindset" & Existence Platform 💡 P2 - 2027
**Philosophy:** Design time, don't just manage it. Life is a system to optimize.

**Platform Reference:** Rob Dyrdek's **Existence** (launched November 2025)
- "Time intelligence platform" that implements the Machine Mindset philosophy
- Tags activities as Life/Work/Health/Sleep with time block tracking
- Uncovers "hidden patterns shaping your life" through qualitative tracking
- **Market Validation:** Major celebrity entrepreneur just launched exactly what we're building!

**Core Mechanics (from Machine Mindset/Existence):**
- **Time Blocking:** Every hour has a purpose (integrate with Calendar)
- **Zero-Based Time Budgeting:** Allocate every minute like a dollar
- **Qualitative Tracking:** Daily 0-10 ratings for Life, Work, Health (5+ years of data)
- **Life Rhythms:** Weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence
- **"Rhythm of Existence" Document:** Guide covering time, health, money, family/work integration

**Implementation Ideas for Neuman Brain:**
- 💡 Daily rating prompts (Life/Work/Health 0-10) - inspired by Existence
- 💡 Qualitative trend visualization (line charts over time)
- 💡 Time block templates (morning routine, deep work, etc.)
- 💡 Weekly reflection prompts
- 💡 Pattern detection across Life/Work/Health categories

**Our Differentiator:** Local-first privacy (vs Existence's likely cloud-based approach)

### 15.2 PMP & ITIL for Personal Life 💡 P2 - 2027
**Philosophy:** Enterprise-grade rigor applied to life management

**PMP (Project Management Professional) Application:**
- 💡 "Life Projects" module (major goals as projects)
  - Project Charter (scope, timeline, budget)
  - Stakeholders (who's involved in this life goal?)
  - Critical Path (dependencies between life tasks)
- 💡 Example: "Buy a house" project with tasks, milestones, deadlines

**ITIL (Service Management) Application:**
- 💡 Personal Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
  - "I agree to workout 3x/week" → track adherence
  - "Service Review" triggers on SLA breach
- 💡 Continuous Improvement loops
  - Retrospectives on failed habits
  - Root cause analysis for missed goals

## Appendix: Feature Parity Methodology

### How Parity Percentages Are Calculated

**Objective:** Provide transparent, verifiable parity calculations to set accurate user expectations.

**Formula:**
```
Parity % = (Shipped Features / Competitor Baseline Features) × 100
```

**Competitor Baseline:** Count of essential features in the competitor's core product tier (not enterprise-only features).

- Hourly rates (❌ Planned Phase 4)
- Invoice generation (❌ Future)
- Idle detection (❌ Future)
- Auto-tracking (❌ Future)
- Rounding rules (❌ Future)
- Team time tracking (❌ Future)
- Integrations (Toggl import) (❌ Future)
- Quick-start from Kanban task (❌ Planned Phase 4)

**Why 71% is accurate:** Phase 3 added all core professional features: manual entry, projects, monthly reports, CSV export, calendar view, and bulk operations. We now have feature parity with Toggl Free tier. Missing only advanced billing (hourly rates, invoicing) and team features.

---

#### AI Terminal: 60% ChatGPT Parity

**Calculation:**
- **ChatGPT Baseline:** 25 core features
- **Neuman Brain Shipped:** 15 features
- **Parity:** 15/25 = 60%

**Missing features (10 total):**
- Voice input
- Voice output
- Image generation (DALL-E)
- Web browsing mode
- Code interpreter
- File uploads
- Custom instructions
- Conversation folders
- Shared conversations
- ChatGPT plugins

**Why 60% is accurate:** We have multi-provider support (8 providers vs ChatGPT's 1), encryption, usage tracking, and auto-fallback. Missing advanced features like voice, images, and plugins.

---

### Verification Process

**How to verify these calculations:**

1. **Open competitor product** (Obsidian, Notion, Jira, etc.)
2. **List all core features** in basic tier (exclude enterprise/plugins)
3. **Count features** in each category
4. **Check Neuman Brain codebase:**
   - Search for implementations
   - Verify in UI (npm run dev)
   - Check task files for "Shipped" status
5. **Calculate:** (Shipped / Total) × 100

**Example for Kanban:**
```bash
# Search for subtask implementation
grep -r "subtask" src/widgets/Kanban/
# Found: SubtaskList.tsx, subtask creation logic, progress tracking

# Search for dependencies
grep -r "dependencies" src/widgets/Kanban/
# Found: dependencies array, blocker logic, visualization

# Search for ListView
ls src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx
# Found: Complete ListView implementation with sorting, filtering, bulk ops
```

**Result:** 49/50 features verified in codebase = 98% parity ✅

---

### Transparency Note

**Why we document methodology:**

1. **Set accurate expectations** - Users know exactly what we have vs don't have
2. **No marketing fluff** - Every percentage is verifiable via codebase
3. **Track progress** - Easy to update as features ship
4. **Honest comparisons** - We don't inflate numbers or cherry-pick competitors
5. **Engineering rigor** - Treat documentation with same precision as code

**Philosophy:** If we claim 98% Kanban parity, you should be able to verify all 49 shipped features by reading the code. If we can't defend a number with evidence, we don't publish it.

---

**Related:**
[Product Roadmap](feature_roadmap.md) - Phased delivery plan
[User Gaps](../strategy/user_expectations_gaps.md) - Feature gaps
[Implementation Plan](../technical/implementation_plan.md) - Sprint breakdown
[CHANGELOG](../CHANGELOG.md) - Release history

**Document Status:** ✅ Active
**Last Updated:** December 14, 2025

  1117→### 2025 Documentation Trends
  1118→- **Docs-as-code**: Treating documentation like code with version control, PRs, CI/CD
  1119→- **AI-enhanced**: Search, content suggestions, auto-generation (GitBook, Mintlify, Notion AI)
  1120→- **API-first**: Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI specs, interactive try-it features
  1121→- **Git-native**: Documentation branches mirror code branches, PR-based reviews
  1122→- **Real-time collaboration**: Multiple editors, Figma-style multiplayer
  1123→- **Changelog automation**: Automatic detection of breaking changes (Bump.sh, Redocly)
  1124→- **SDK generation**: Auto-generate client libraries alongside documentation
  1125→- **Single-source-of-truth**: API gateway generates docs automatically

  1278→### Web Application Security
  1279→- Burp Suite - Industry standard web security testing
  1280→  - Community Edition (Free): Proxy, Repeater, basic manual testing
  1281→  - Professional: Advanced scanner, intruder, automated testing
  1282→  - Enterprise: Continuous automated scanning at scale
  1283→  - Note: Steep learning curve, resource-intensive (3500MB+ when idle)
  1284→- OWASP ZAP (Zed Attack Proxy) - Free, open-source, SSP-maintained
  1285→  - Man-in-the-middle proxy, automated scanner
  1286→  - Available for all major OS, daemon mode
  1287→  - Highly customizable with plugins/scripts
  1288→- Caido - Modern web security toolkit, now in Kali Linux 2025.3
  1289→  - Web-based alternative to Burp Suite
  1290→  - Freemium model, modern interface
  1291→- Nikto - Open-source web server scanner
  1292→  - Detects dangerous files, outdated configs, vulnerabilities
  1293→- Wapiti - Black-box web application scanner
  1294→  - Fuzzer-based vulnerability detection
  1295→  - SQL injection, XSS, file inclusion testing
  1296→- Skipfish - Automated web reconnaissance and security audit
  1297→- SQLMap - Automated SQL injection detection and exploitation

  2410→================================================================================
  2411→## UX/UI BEST-IN-CLASS EXAMPLES
  2412→================================================================================
  2413→
  2414→### Design Philosophy Leaders
  2415→| App | Key UX Innovation | Why It Works |
  2416→|-----|-------------------|--------------|
  2417→| Superhuman | Keyboard-first, speed | 100ms target response time |
  2418→| Linear | Clean, focused, opinionated | Removes decision fatigue |
  2419→| Notion | Block-based, modular | Infinite flexibility |
  2420→| Things 3 | Tactile animations | Emotional satisfaction |
  2421→| Arc Browser | Spaces, sidebar | Reimagined navigation |
  2422→| Craft | Native feel, beautiful | Apple design language |
  2423→| Todoist | Natural language input | Zero-friction capture |
  2424→| Motion | AI-first scheduling | Removes manual planning |
  2425→| **Fizzy** | Vibrant colors, Do Bar | Anti-muted UI, keyboard-driven commands |
  2426→
  2427→### Design Principles to Learn From
  2428→- **Superhuman**: Speed is a feature. Target 100ms response.
  2429→- **Linear**: Opinionated > flexible. Reduce choices.
  2430→- **Notion**: Simplify the complicated. Minimal interface.
  2431→- **Things 3**: Delight in interaction. Micro-animations matter.
  2432→- **Arc**: Challenge conventions. Sidebar > top tabs.
  2433→- **Craft**: Platform-native > cross-platform generic.
  2434→- **Obsidian**: Extensibility via plugins, core stays simple.
  2435→- **Figma**: Collaboration as first-class feature.

# Neuman Brain - Competitive Landscape & Alternatives Research
# Last Updated: December 7, 2025
# Purpose: Comprehensive list of productivity tools to inform feature development
# Categories: 66+ | Tools Listed: 1,250+ | Lines: 2,720+

================================================================================
## CURRENT FEATURE PARITY (from README.md)
================================================================================
Category        | Competitor       | Parity
----------------|------------------|--------
Notes           | Obsidian         | 55%
Notes           | Notion           | 50%
Tasks/PM        | Jira/Linear      | 98%
Time Tracking   | Toggl            | 74%
Calendar        | Google Calendar  | 65%
Dashboard       | Notion           | 70%

================================================================================
## NOTES / KNOWLEDGE MANAGEMENT / PKM
================================================================================

### Manual Additions for Review
(Empty - items have been processed and categorized into appropriate sections below)

### Major Players
- Notion - All-in-one workspace, databases, wikis
- Obsidian - Local-first, graph-based, markdown, plugins
- Evernote - Classic note-taking, web clipper
- Apple Notes - Native Apple ecosystem
- Microsoft OneNote - Microsoft ecosystem, free-form canvas
- Google Keep - Simple notes, Google integration

### PKM-Focused Tools
- Logseq - Outliner-based, open source, privacy-first
- Roam Research - Networked thought, $15/month
- Capacities - Object-based PKM approach
- Anytype - Local-first, open source, E2E encrypted
- Tana - Outliner with supertags
- Reflect Notes - Networked thought, AI-enhanced
- Mem - AI-powered notes and search
- Heptabase - Visual canvas PKM
- Scrintal - Visual canvas notes
- Kosmik - Visual workspace for PKM

### Markdown/Plain-Text Focused
- Bear - Minimalist, Apple ecosystem
- Simplenote - Cross-platform, simple
- Joplin - Open source, E2E encryption
- Notesnook - Privacy-focused, E2E encrypted
- Standard Notes - E2E encrypted
- Zettlr - Academic/research with citations
- iA Writer - Distraction-free writing
- Typora - WYSIWYG markdown
- Craft - Apple-focused, beautiful design

### Research & Academic
- Zotero - Reference management
- Mendeley - Academic reference manager
- RemNote - Spaced repetition + PKM
- Supernotes - Notecard-based structure

### Team/Collaborative Notes
- Slite - Team knowledge base
- Notejoy - Team notes
- Nuclino - Team wiki/knowledge base
- Coda - Docs + spreadsheets + apps
- Quip - Salesforce ecosystem

### Self-Hosted/Open Source
- TiddlyWiki - Single-file wiki
- Trilium Next - Hierarchical notes, self-hosted
- AFFiNE - Open source Notion alternative
- AppFlowy - Open source, local-first
- Outline - Team knowledge base
- Turtl - E2E encrypted notes

### Digital Garden / Zettelkasten
- Neuron - Zettelkasten publishing
- Quartz - Publish Obsidian/Roam gardens
- Dendron - Hierarchical note-taking for devs
- Foam - VS Code-based PKM

### AI-Enhanced Second Brain
- Saner.AI - AI-powered organization
- Fabric.so - AI-native file organizer
- Amplenote - Notes + tasks + calendar
- Walling - Visual brainstorming

### AI Research Assistants / Notebooks
- Google NotebookLM - AI-powered research assistant, Gemini-powered, source-grounded responses
  - Deep Research feature (Nov 2025) - automated multi-source research reports
  - Audio Overviews - converts documents to podcast-like discussions
  - Video Overviews - transforms documents into visual presentations
  - Mind Maps - visual connections between topics
  - Study tools - flashcards, quizzes, study guides
  - Supports: Google Docs/Sheets/Slides, PDFs, Markdown, URLs, YouTube, audio files
  - Free tier available; NotebookLM Plus via Google One AI Premium ($20/mo)
- Elicit - AI research assistant for academic papers
- Consensus - AI search engine for scientific research
- Perplexity - AI-powered research and search
- SciSpace - AI copilot for research papers
- Semantic Scholar - AI-powered research tool
- Connected Papers - Visual citation explorer
- Research Rabbit - Research discovery tool

### AI-Native Workspaces / Canvas (NEW - Dec 2025)
- **Refly AI (refly.ai)** - Open-source AI-native creation engine, "Vibe Workflow" platform
  - **Launched:** January 2025 | **Current Version:** v0.10.0 (Dec 2025)
  - **GitHub:** 4,900+ stars, 438 forks, 33+ contributors (very active)
  - **License:** ReflyAI Open Source License (Apache 2.0 with restrictions)
  - **Positioning:** Combines Notion + NotebookLM + Perplexity in one workspace
  - **Core Features:**
    - Free canvas interface with multi-threaded dialogues
    - AI knowledge base with RAG (semantic search)
    - 13+ LLM integrations (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini 2.0, DeepSeek R1)
    - Visual workflow builder ("Vibe Workflow" - natural language automation)
    - Chrome extension for web clipping
    - WYSIWYG AI editor with context-aware prompting
    - Workflow marketplace with creator monetization (20% revenue share)
  - **Tech Stack:** TypeScript 97%, React, NestJS, Zustand, Tiptap, ReactFlow, LangChain
  - **Deployment:** Cloud (refly.ai), Self-hosted (Docker), Kubernetes
  - **Pricing:** Freemium (Free + Pro + Team), BYOK option, Credits-based
  - **Strengths:** Multi-model AI, visual workflows, active OSS community, self-hostable
  - **Weaknesses:** Cloud-dependent, credit costs, learning curve, "good enough everywhere"
  - **vs Neuman Brain:** Content creation focus vs productivity focus, cloud vs local-first
  - **Sources:** [XDA Article](https://www.xda-developers.com/refly-ai-combines-notion-notebooklm-perplexity-in-one-super-productivity-app/), [GitHub](https://github.com/refly-ai/refly)
- AFFiNE - Open source Notion + Miro alternative, local-first canvas
- Heptabase - Visual canvas PKM for thinking and learning
- Scrintal - Visual canvas notes for connected ideas
- Kosmik - Visual workspace for PKM with spatial organization
- tldraw - Open source infinite canvas whiteboard
- Excalidraw - Hand-drawn style whiteboard, open source

### Handwriting/Drawing Notes
- GoodNotes - iPad, handwriting
- Notability - iPad, audio sync
- Comma note
- Hypernotes

================================================================================
## TASKS / PROJECT MANAGEMENT
================================================================================

### Major Players
- Asana - Team project management
- Monday.com - Work OS, visual workflows
- Jira - Atlassian, dev teams, agile
- Linear - Modern issue tracking
- Trello - Kanban boards
- ClickUp - All-in-one workspace
- Basecamp - Simple project management
- Wrike - Enterprise PM

### Personal Task Management
- Todoist - GTD-friendly, cross-platform
- Things 3 - Apple ecosystem, beautiful
- TickTick - Feature-rich, Pomodoro
- Microsoft To Do - Microsoft ecosystem
- Any.do - Simple, cross-platform
- Remember the Milk - Classic GTD
- OmniFocus - Apple, GTD power users
- Google Tasks - Simple, Google integrated
- Apple Reminders - Native Apple

### AI-Powered Planners
- Motion - AI scheduling and planning
- Akiflow - Time blocking + tasks
- Sunsama - Mindful daily planning
- Reclaim.ai - AI calendar management

### Open Source / Self-Hosted
- Vikunja - Self-hosted Todoist alternative
- OpenProject - Enterprise, open source
- Taiga - Agile PM, open source
- Focalboard - Open source Trello/Notion
- Planka - Open source Trello alternative
- Kanboard - Minimalist Kanban
- WeKan - Open source Kanban
- Super Productivity - Offline-first, integrations
- **Fizzy** - 37signals' open source Kanban (O'Saasy License, self-hostable, Rails + Hotwire)

### Developer-Focused
- GitHub Projects
- GitLab Issues
- Gitea
- YouTrack - JetBrains
- Shortcut (formerly Clubhouse)
- Zenhub - GitHub integration
- Pivotal Tracker

### Enterprise/Complex
- Smartsheet - Spreadsheet-style PM
- Teamwork - Client work
- Hive - Productivity platform
- ProofHub - All-in-one PM
- nTask - Simple for small business
- Backlog - Agile + version control
- GanttPRO - Gantt chart focused
- MS Project - Microsoft enterprise

### Specialized
- Height - Modern Linear alternative
- Plane - Open source project management
- Fibery - Work management
- Workflowy - Outliner/lists
- Dynalist - Outliner
- Quire - Nested task lists

### Kanban-Specific
- **Fizzy (fizzy.do)** - 37signals (Basecamp makers), open source (O'Saasy License)
  - Ruby on Rails + Hotwire, SQLite/MySQL, self-hostable
  - "Kanban as it should be" - fast, vibrant, no AI bloat
  - Key features: auto-close old cards, webhooks (Slack/Campfire), notification stacks
  - **Golden Ticket system**: high-priority cards float to top with golden glow
  - **Do Bar**: command-line style keyboard-driven interface (like Cmd+K)
  - Distinctive visual design: saturated colors, gradients, texture (anti-muted-UI)
  - Free: 1,000 cards (lifetime), Paid: $20/mo unlimited cards/users
  - GitHub: 3K+ stars, 334 forks, 37 contributors (Dec 2025)
  - Competitive insight: simplified from Basecamp's Card Tables, focused workflow
  - O'Saasy License: free to use/modify/self-host, cannot compete as SaaS
- KanbanFlow - Time tracking built-in
- Kanbanchi - Google Workspace
- MeisterTask - Visual, team-oriented
- Kanbanize - Enterprise Kanban
- LeanKit - Enterprise Kanban

### CLI-Based
- Taskwarrior - GTD-style, scriptable
- Ultralist - Simple CLI tasks
- todo.txt - Plain text tasks

### Legacy/Other
- Trac
- Redmine
- Mantis BT
- Easy Redmine
- DevRev
- Brisqi
- WorkLenz
- Upbase

================================================================================
## HELPDESK / TICKETING / SUPPORT
================================================================================

### Open Source / Self-Hosted
- osTicket - Popular open-source helpdesk, PHP-based, email piping
- FreeScout - Free, open-source Zendesk alternative, lightweight (10MB), shared hosting compatible
- Zammad - Modern open-source helpdesk, beautiful UI, multi-channel (email, phone, chat, social)
- UVdesk - Comprehensive open-source helpdesk, Laravel-based, ecommerce focused
- Request Tracker (RT) - Veteran open-source, 20+ years, government/enterprise grade
- Frappe Helpdesk - Modern, ERPNext ecosystem, extensible
- HESK - Simple cloud-based helpdesk with knowledge base
- Faveo Helpdesk - Laravel-based, multiple editions (Community, Pro, Service Desk)

### IT Service Management (ITSM)
- GLPI - Open-source ITSM + IT Asset Management, plugin ecosystem
- iTop - ITIL-compliant, low-code CMDB, change/incident/problem management
- OTRS Community Edition - Open-source ticketing, now part of EasyVista
- OpenITSM - ITIL-compliant, built on i-doit framework
- KIX - German open-source ITSM, ticket + asset management
- ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus - Free community edition for SMBs

### Commercial/Enterprise
- Zendesk - Industry leader, omnichannel support
- Freshdesk - Freshworks suite, affordable
- Intercom - Conversational support, AI-powered
- HubSpot Service Hub - CRM-integrated support
- Salesforce Service Cloud - Enterprise, Einstein AI
- Jira Service Management - Atlassian, free for 3 agents, ITIL-certified
- ServiceNow - Enterprise ITSM leader
- Help Scout - Email-based, simple
- Kayako - Unified customer service
- Front - Team inbox, collaborative email

## DOCUMENTATION PLATFORMS / DEVELOPER DOCS
================================================================================

### Static Site Generators (Docs-as-Code)
- Docusaurus - Meta-backed, React + Markdown, versioning, i18n, Algolia search (Free, OSS)
- MkDocs - Python-based, Material theme popular, simple setup (Free, OSS)
- Sphinx - Python docs standard, reStructuredText/Markdown, PDF output (Free, OSS)
- Hugo - Fastest SSG, Go-based, 400+ themes, multilingual (Free, OSS)
- Jekyll - Ruby, GitHub Pages native, mature ecosystem (Free, OSS)
- VuePress - Vue.js-powered, Vue 2, now community-maintained (Free, OSS)
- VitePress - VuePress successor, Vue 3 + Vite, <100ms hot reload, Vue team supported (Free, OSS)
- mdBook - Rust-based, Markdown to HTML/PDF/ePub, used by Rust docs (Free, OSS)
- Astro Starlight - Astro-powered, modern, fast, excellent DX (Free, OSS)
- Read the Docs - Hosts Sphinx/MkDocs docs, auto-builds from Git (Free OSS, paid private)

### Hosted/SaaS Documentation Platforms
- GitBook - Git-based workflow, AI search, WYSIWYG, developer-focused (Free personal, paid teams)
- Mintlify - Modern, AI-powered, beautiful design, MDX support (Free 80 articles, $150/mo paid)
- ReadMe - Interactive API docs, metrics, developer hub, guides + recipes ($99/mo startup, $399/mo enterprise)
- Document360 - Internal knowledge bases, governance, analytics (Paid)
- Archbee - Team docs, 1800+ integrations, AI search ($200/mo, add-ons $100/mo)
- Notion - All-in-one workspace, limited publishing, needs 3rd party for custom domains (Free tier, $10/mo+)
- Confluence - Atlassian suite, enterprise, Jira integration, internal-focused (Free 10 users, $5.75/user/mo)
- Nuclino - Clean simple interface, graph view, AI assistant (Free tier, paid plans)
- Slite - Remote/async-first, AI-powered, internal knowledge (Paid)
- Tettra - Simple wiki for teams, Slack integration (Paid)
- DeveloperHub.io - Hosted docs platform, beautiful, low setup (Paid)
- Notion - All-in-one workspace, wikis, limited public publishing (Free tier, paid teams)
- KnowledgeOwl - Customer-facing knowledge base, analytics (Paid)

### API Documentation Tools
- Swagger UI / SwaggerHub - Industry standard, OpenAPI visualization, try-it-out (Free OSS / Paid hosted)
- Stoplight Elements - Stripe-like docs, React component, OpenAPI (Free tier, $79/mo+)
- Redocly - Enterprise-grade, docs-as-code, CLI tools, multi-team ($10/mo Pro, $24/mo Enterprise)
- Scalar - Open-source, best "Try It" feature, Git-native, dark-mode, JetBrains Mono (Free OSS, hosted options)
- Bump.sh - OpenAPI + AsyncAPI support, Stripe-like 3-column layout, changelog detection (SaaS pricing)
- Zuplo - GitOps approach, API management + docs combined, RateMyOpenAPI tool (Integrated pricing)
- Slate - Open-source, beautiful three-panel layout, Ruby-based (Free, OSS)
- Postman - API platform with docs publishing, collections (Free tier, paid plans)
- Apidog - API design, documentation, testing all-in-one (Freemium)

### Self-Hosted / Open Source Documentation
- BookStack - Books/chapters structure, WYSIWYG, role-based permissions (PHP/Laravel/MySQL, MIT)
- Wiki.js - Modern, Git sync, multiple editors, elegant UI (Node.js, MIT)
- Docmost - Enterprise-ready, Confluence/Notion alternative, SSO, offline-capable (Modern stack, OSS)
- Outline - Real-time collaboration, Slack integration, minimal UI (Node.js, requires config)
- DokuWiki - No database needed, plain text files, simple syntax, easy maintenance (PHP)
- MediaWiki - Wikipedia's engine, powerful, extensible, multilingual (PHP)
- XWiki - Enterprise features, Java-based, templates, strict access controls (Java)
- TiddlyWiki - Single HTML file, portable, personal wiki (JavaScript)
- Documize - Wiki + documentation hybrid, self/cloud/hybrid hosting options (Go)

### Office/Document Platforms
- Google Docs - Collaborative, Google ecosystem, easy sharing (Free / Workspace)
- Proton Docs - Privacy-first, E2E encrypted, no tracking (Proton plans)
- Dropbox Paper - Collaborative docs, Dropbox integration (Dropbox plans)
- Coda - Docs + databases + automation, powerful formulas (Free tier, paid)
- Craft - Apple-focused, beautiful native design (Free tier, $5/mo+)
- Microsoft Word/Loop - Microsoft ecosystem, Loop for modern collaboration (M365 plans)
- Quip - Salesforce ecosystem, docs + spreadsheets combined (Salesforce plans)

### 2025 Documentation Trends
- **Docs-as-code**: Treating documentation like code with version control, PRs, CI/CD
- **AI-enhanced**: Search, content suggestions, auto-generation (GitBook, Mintlify, Notion AI)
- **API-first**: Auto-generated docs from OpenAPI specs, interactive try-it features
- **Git-native**: Documentation branches mirror code branches, PR-based reviews
- **Real-time collaboration**: Multiple editors, Figma-style multiplayer
- **Changelog automation**: Automatic detection of breaking changes (Bump.sh, Redocly)
- **SDK generation**: Auto-generate client libraries alongside documentation
- **Single-source-of-truth**: API gateway generates docs automatically

### Documentation Platform Comparison Matrix
| Platform | Best For | Hosting | Pricing |
|----------|----------|---------|---------|
| Docusaurus | Open source projects | Self-hosted | Free |
| GitBook | Dev teams, mixed technical | Hosted | Freemium |
| Mintlify | SaaS, modern aesthetic | Hosted | $150/mo |
| ReadMe | API providers, DevRel | Hosted | $99-399/mo |
| Confluence | Enterprise, internal | Hosted/Server | $5.75/user |
| Notion | Flexible teams | Hosted | $10/mo+ |
| VitePress | Vue ecosystem, fast | Self-hosted | Free |
| Redocly | Enterprise API docs | Hybrid | $10-24/mo |
| BookStack | Self-hosted wikis | Self-hosted | Free |

## SECURITY TESTING / PENETRATION TESTING
================================================================================

### Web Application Security
- Burp Suite - Industry standard web security testing
  - Community Edition (Free): Proxy, Repeater, basic manual testing
  - Professional: Advanced scanner, intruder, automated testing
  - Enterprise: Continuous automated scanning at scale
  - Note: Steep learning curve, resource-intensive (3500MB+ when idle)
- OWASP ZAP (Zed Attack Proxy) - Free, open-source, SSP-maintained
  - Man-in-the-middle proxy, automated scanner
  - Available for all major OS, daemon mode
  - Highly customizable with plugins/scripts
- Caido - Modern web security toolkit, now in Kali Linux 2025.3
  - Web-based alternative to Burp Suite
  - Freemium model, modern interface
- Nikto - Open-source web server scanner
  - Detects dangerous files, outdated configs, vulnerabilities
- Wapiti - Black-box web application scanner
  - Fuzzer-based vulnerability detection
  - SQL injection, XSS, file inclusion testing
- Skipfish - Automated web reconnaissance and security audit
- SQLMap - Automated SQL injection detection and exploitation
- Nuclei - Fast vulnerability scanner with templates

### Commercial DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing)
- Invicti (formerly Netsparker) - Proof-based scanning, minimal false positives
- Acunetix - Fast, accurate, API testing support
- Qualys WAS - Cloud-based, enterprise scale
- StackHawk - Developer-centric, CI/CD native
- Checkmarx - Full SAST/DAST/SCA platform

### Kali Linux Browser-Based Tools
- Kali Linux Browser VM (Azure) - 2500+ security tools via browser GUI
  - No VNC client required
  - Includes: Nmap, Metasploit, Wireshark, Aircrack-ng, John the Ripper
- New in Kali 2025.3:
  - llm-tools-nmap - AI-powered network scanning
  - mcp-kali-server - AI agent integration
  - Caido/Caido-cli - Web security auditing

### Network Security
- Nmap - Network discovery and security auditing
- Wireshark - Network protocol analyzer
- Metasploit Framework - Penetration testing framework
- Nessus - Vulnerability scanner
- OpenVAS - Open-source vulnerability scanner

### Password/Credential Testing
- John the Ripper - Password cracker
- Hashcat - Advanced password recovery
- Hydra - Network login cracker

### Design Philosophy Leaders
| App | Key UX Innovation | Why It Works |
|-----|-------------------|--------------|
| Superhuman | Keyboard-first, speed | 100ms target response time |
| Linear | Clean, focused, opinionated | Removes decision fatigue |
| Notion | Block-based, modular | Infinite flexibility |
| Things 3 | Tactile animations | Emotional satisfaction |
| Arc Browser | Spaces, sidebar | Reimagined navigation |
| Craft | Native feel, beautiful | Apple design language |
| Todoist | Natural language input | Zero-friction capture |
| Motion | AI-first scheduling | Removes manual planning |
| **Fizzy** | Vibrant colors, Do Bar | Anti-muted UI, keyboard-driven commands |

### Design Principles to Learn From
- **Superhuman**: Speed is a feature. Target 100ms response.
- **Linear**: Opinionated > flexible. Reduce choices.
- **Notion**: Simplify the complicated. Minimal interface.
- **Things 3**: Delight in interaction. Micro-animations matter.
- **Arc**: Challenge conventions. Sidebar > top tabs.
- **Craft**: Platform-native > cross-platform generic.
- **Obsidian**: Extensibility via plugins, core stays simple.
- **Figma**: Collaboration as first-class feature.
- **Fizzy**: Vibrant > muted. Stand out visually. Command-line UI (Do Bar) for power users.

## NOTES FOR DEVELOPMENT
================================================================================

### High-Priority Feature Gaps (categories with limited Neuman Brain coverage):
1. Habit Tracking - No current implementation
